title: GCD

content.problemStatement:
Write a program to find GCD of two integers by using recursive functions.

The Greatest Common Divisor (GCD), also known as the Greatest Common Factor (GCF), of two integers is the largest number that divides both of them without leaving a remainder. In other words, it's the greatest number that both numbers share as a divisor.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space-separated integers a and b (1 ≤ a, b ≤ 1000).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line in the format "GCD: X" where X is the greatest common divisor of a and b.

sampleTestCases.explanation: GCDs are computed as follows: gcd(12,18)=6, gcd(100,25)=25, gcd(7,13)=1.
Both numbers are 1, so the GCD is 1.
Both numbers are equal to 1000, therefore the GCD equals 1000.

solution.approach: We use the classic Euclidean algorithm implemented recursively. For two numbers a and b (a ≥ b), gcd(a,b)=gcd(b,a mod b). The recursion stops when b becomes 0, at which point a is the GCD. This runs in O(log min(a,b)) time and uses only O(log min(a,b)) stack depth. For each test case we apply the recursive function and print the result in the required format.
solution.timeComplexity: O(log min(a,b)) per test case, O(T·log max(a,b)) total
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides recursion stack (O(log min(a,b)) call depth)

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{private static int gcd(int a,int b){if(b==0)return a;return gcd(b,a%b);}public static void main(String[] args) throws IOException{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());for(int i=0;i<T;i++){StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());int a=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());int b=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());sb.append("GCD: ").append(gcd(a,b));if(i<T-1)sb.append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
